title: RunForge Cancellation Marker
description: Marker file written under a run directory when training is cancelled via SIGTERM and Python catches the signal + completes graceful cleanup before exit. Mirrors the .index-orphan marker shape (single writer = Python provenance/cancel module; single reader = TS Bridge run-manager + observability). Pairs with the run_cancelled event in events.schema.v1. Filename: <run_dir>/.cancelled. Absence of this marker after a cancel-then-non-zero-exit indicates SIGKILL or crash-during-cancel; TS Bridge surfaces accordingly.

schema_version.description: Versioned schema id. Increment on shape changes.

run_id.description: The run id whose training was cancelled. Permissive identifier shape (matches both TS run-folder.ts 4-segment form and Python metadata.generate_run_id 3-segment form per F-COORD-012 — to be unified in Phase 5+).

run_dir.description: Workspace-relative path to the run directory. Forward slashes only (Windows backslashes normalized at write time).

cancelled_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when graceful cleanup completed and marker was atomically written.

step.description: Which run phase the cancel was caught during. UI uses this to surface what work was lost (e.g. 'cancelled during training — no metrics' vs 'cancelled during artifact writing — partial artifacts may exist').

reason.description: Optional human-readable trigger (e.g., 'user cancelled via VS Code progress UI', 'workspace closed', 'timeout').

partial_artifacts.description: Workspace-relative paths to any artifacts that were partially or fully written before cancellation completed. Lets the UI offer 'Open partial artifacts' affordance instead of treating the run dir as empty.
